page:
# Day 1: Welcome to the Sea
## Focus
Marine biology as a discipline, why the ocean matters, and how this course will work.
## Learning Objectives
- Describe the scope of marine biology. - Identify ecological and economic reasons the ocean matters. - Connect the history of marine science to present-day research questions.
## Core Topics
### Marine Biology in Context
Marine biology examines organisms, habitats, and ecological processes across the global ocean. It overlaps with ecology, chemistry, geology, climatology, and policy.
### Why the Ocean Matters
- The ocean regulates climate and stores heat. - Marine systems support biodiversity and global food webs. - Coastal economies depend on fisheries, tourism, shipping, and recreation.
### A Brief History of the Field
Trace how marine biology developed from exploration and specimen collection into a modern science driven by remote sensing, submersibles, molecular tools, and long-term ecological monitoring.
## Applied Activity
Write a short course entry in your lab notebook:
1. Define marine biology in your own words. 2. List three reasons ocean systems matter locally or globally. 3. Identify one marine ecosystem you want to learn more about and why.
## Exit Question
What makes marine biology different from studying terrestrial ecosystems alone?
